meaning: adj.准备不足的,准备不充分的
examples: I hear a lot of people make that mistake and go in underprepared.
Afterreviewing her plans, judges ruled Laura was underprepared for such a voyage.
He put a lot of work into a big speech in Cairo but a visit to the Saudi king was underprepared and he never visited Israel at all.
And those who do make it to college often find themselves underprepared for its rigors.
Why are the leading players underprepared for the biggest Test series of the year?
